mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: SEE the Error: Standard Error Estimates variability, Imagine the Standard Error (SE) as the 'Standard' amount of 'Error' you'd expect in your sample statistic, like the mean.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: Standard error measures the variability or precision of a sample statistic (like the mean) across different samples from the same population.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: It is calculated by dividing the standard deviation (σ) by the square root of the sample size (n): SE = σ / √n.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: A smaller standard error indicates that the sample statistic is a more precise estimate of the population parameter.
